_meta.method: §6.5 fresh-context agent fan-out — 20 fresh Opus agents, one per query, each given only the anonymized description (verbatim, no skill name) + the single query; pure relevance question; verdicts adjudicated from each agent's reasoning, not the raw token.
_meta.description_measured: Assign the right Requiem perks to a new or modded NPC via houseCARL — derive the source-carried perk set (weapon mastery, block, heavy armor, evasion, magic-school nodes) from the actor's equipment, spell kit, and tier against Requiem's own template ladders, write it as rank-chain PerkPlacements, and disposition a mod's own PERK records. Use when the user wants to give an NPC or follower Requiem perks, fix a boss or enemy whose perks are missing or wrong, build a perk loadout for a custom warrior, archer, mage, or spellsword from its gear and spells, ask which perks an NPC should carry in Requiem, or handle a mod's custom perk records. Never hand-place Reqtificator-stamped perks — load this before writing any Perks list, not after the build pass double-stamps the actor.

results.reasoning_gist: follower with zero perks = the missing-perks case named in the description
boss with vanilla Armsman = 'fix a boss whose perks are wrong', central match
warlock perks from its spells = mage loadout from spell kit
per-record disposition of a mod's PERK records, named explicitly
archer loadouts from gear = the gear-derivation method; plurality doesn't change scope
the Reqtificator-vs-hand-place boundary question is the doctrine the capability owns
spellsword mix of 1H + destruction = the named hybrid case

MISS — judge read the smithing-yield perk clash as a crafting-economy/system question, not PERK disposition; conceded the custom-perk-record angle 'brushes the edge'. Borderline; the disposition lane (balance-bearing overlap → flag) is exactly this query
draugr variants = enemy perk assignment
knight loadout from greatsword/heavy/shield = the equipment formula
whole-NPC frame (level/class/factions/outfit) correctly routed to NPC patching
designing a new perk tree = authoring, which the capability never does
SPID distribution mechanism, no derivation intent
player build advice, no actor record or authoring task
HalfCostPerk = spell-record wiring (magic domain); overlap is only the word 'perk'
spell cost/magnitude = magic balancing, different domain
racial abilities/resistances = RACE-record properties, race patching
SkyPatcher ini = runtime authoring, wrong output medium, no derivation

MISFIRE — judge argued a shield-carrying enemy that never blocks plausibly has a missing Block perk chain, so the skill should load to diagnose. Substantive reasoning; acceptable low-cost overtrigger under the §3.3 asymmetric-cost doctrine (intended answer: combat style → NPC patching)
broken description strings = cosmetic text fix, no Requiem balance
